BREAKING: Solana processed a record $650 billion in Stablecoin transactions in February 2026.

As a result, aggregate Stablecoin transaction volume is now nearly a record $2 trillion per month.

Stablecoin volumes on Solana nearly TRIPLED month-over-month, with another surge https://t.co/hGB23pwsxK

result.text:
BREAKING: Solana processed a record $650 billion in Stablecoin transactions in February 2026.

As a result, aggregate Stablecoin transaction volume is now nearly a record $2 trillion per month.

Stablecoin volumes on Solana nearly TRIPLED month-over-month, with another surge expected in March amid the Iran War.

The surge in volume comes after the launch of Western Union's $USDPT, Jupiter's $JUPUSD, which has gained traction amid its goal of returning a yield back to the ecosystem.

To put this into perspective, CME Group futures trading in gold just hit a record $208 billion per month.

In other words, Stablecoin transaction volumes are now nearly 9 TIMES the size of gold futures traded on CME.

tweets.full_text:
The rise and fall of Tensor

2022:
> two ex-quant traders with no funding
> win two Solana hackathons back to back
> bootstrap with $60k prize money
> launch during the FTX collapse
> zero users, zero revenue, zero social presence

2023:
> raise $3M
> quietly become #1 NFT marketplace on Solana
> beat Magic Eden in volume
> launch Tensorians NFT at 1.69 SOL

late 2023:
> Solana season begins
> pioneering cNFTs
> Tensorians hit 85 SOL (50x from mint)
> capturing 85% of all Solana NFT volume
> the "Blur of Solana" narrative is real

2024:
> TNSR token launches over $1B FDV
> everyone celebrates
> volumes go down
> farmers leave Tensor for Magic Eden

2025:
> NFT market dies
> daily volume down 80% from peak
> $20k daily fees
> team builds a new platform (Vector)
> pivot more and more to memecoins
> Vector adopts $TNSR (new hope)

2025 (Coinbase acquisition):
> Coinbase acquired Vector just to shut it down
> Coinbase takes over core team
> Tensor and $TNSR token not part of the deal
> $TNSR pumped 8-11x before announcement
> founder allocations were relocked for additional 3 years

2026:
> Tensor foundation leads marketplace now
> last tweet 4 months ago
> revenue down to $250/day
> future unclear...

tweets.full_text:
🎙️ Solana Podcasts

To stay in sync with @solana news, opportunities, and learn how others operate, follow pages who consistently share insights:

< Community Voices >

1. Validated {@ValidatedPod}
→ hosted by @Austin_Federa
Top builders and experts break down deep Web3 concepts and Solana ecosystem developments

2. The Index Podcast {@theindexshow}
→ hosted by @afkehaya and @NickyScanz
Alex and Nicky talk with founders, developers, and investors from the ecosystem.

3. Lightspeed {@LightspeedpodHQ}
→ hosted by @defi_kay_
Breakdown of how crypto can solve real problems and create products users love.

4. Solfate Podcast {@SolfatePod}
→ hosted by @jamesrp13 & @nickfrosty
Interviews with founders and builders on Solana.

5. Talking Tokens {@_TalkingTokens}
→ hosted by @jacqmelinek
Focused on interviews with crypto leaders, startups, market participants, and up-and-coming founders changing the industry.

6. SOL BROTHERS {@SolBrothersPod}
→ hosted by @YouKnowEno & @simonmolitor
Eno and Simon dissects the latest apps, major ecosystem developments, and founder perspectives from the front lines.

7. Exploring Solana
→ hosted by @mango_
Mango invites founders and employees of top Solana projects to talk about their background and projects past and future.

< Solana Broadcast Network > by @SolanaFndn

8. Billions
→ hosted by @Pedromiranda
Pedro interviews founders, exploring how they design, grow, and sustain mainstream crypto products and communities.

9. Bits to Bricks
→ hosted by @amiravalliani
Amira breaks down DePIN, focusing on how the networks work and why they’re growing.

10. Open Intelligence
→ hosted by @_rishinsharma
Rishin covers AI and blockchain on Solana, focusing on open intelligence and the builders behind it.

11. On The Road
→ hosted by @nickducoff
Nick breaks down finance moving onchain, focusing on markets, products, and institutions on Solana.

12. Solana is Global
→ hosted by @afscott
Alex highlights how Solana is expanding globally, sharing local stories from Europe, Middle East, and Asia.

13. The Stack
→ hosted by @nocircuit
Ilan shares how Solana products are built, the challenges faced, and key technical decisions behind them.

Check the full list via the link below 👇

tweets.full_text:
Weekly Update – @NOMADZxyz | Week 14 [2026]  

1.1 Quick wins: 
1. Went live with 10th release with AI agent at Solana dApp Store
2. Launched aggregator v.2 and added additional information for "My Trips"
3. Designed and shipped discount codes for events tickets
4. Had a launch campaign with RadiantsDAO and cohort 2 of Nomadz 100
5. Crossed 17.3K registered users
6. Increase retention rate from 26% to 28%

1.2 What's next: 
1. Platform: update badges, referral program with tiers, launch discounts for communities, an aggregator v.2 
2. Mobile: Launch v. 11 with events globe, referral and more
3. Partnership campaign with @cherrydotfun 

2. Highlights  
2.1 Product - Platform 
1. Improved aggregator with v.2. 
2. Discount codes for events tickets
3. Improved "My Trips" with additional information   

2.2 Product - @solanamobile  
1. Went live with release #10 with AI agent integration, new quests and badges. 
2. Release #11 in progress: Communities verification + new badges  

2.3 Growth
1. Recorded podcasts with @afscott and @theindexshow with @NickyScanz 
2. Launched a campaign with @RadiantsDAO 
3. Sent the third email campaign: 17,000 emails delivered. 
4. Crossed 17,300+ registered users and 19,900+ followers on X. 
5. Launched cohort 2 of Nomadz 100 with 16 new creators. Hosted a closed event for them.
6. Colaborated with new creators: @lightofrahim and @MagicSoulana 

3. Lowlights  
1. We were not accepted to @nitrodotacc 
2. Still negotiating 2 major partnerships, need to close them asap

4. Core Metrics  
1. Total Users: 17,303 (↑ from 17,072 last week) 
2. Active Users (week): 787 (↑ from 834 last week) 
3. New Users (week):  172 (↑ from 266 last week) 
4. DAU: ~113 on average (↑ from 120 last week) 
5. Transactions on Solana (week): ~6420 (↑ from 3425 last week) 
6. X Followers (@NOMADZxyz): 19,915+ (↑ from 19,835)  

5. This Week's Focus  
→ Launch discount codes for communities
→ Improve badges, discount codes, events calendar with side events 
→ Launch Nomadz 100 Cohort 2 and have the second community call 
→ New partnerships and launch campaigns with @cherrydotfun and @perena 

 6. Asks  
1. Looking for companies actively traveling to help them save on hotels and provide concierge service  

7. Shoutouts 
Big thanks to the @lagunacarta for connecting with podcasts and having a call with me. Grateful for my team, clients and partners for support and trust. 

We will ship more this week!

Weekly Update – @NOMADZxyz | Week 13 [2026]  

1.1 Quick wins: 
1. Launched events in a globe format 
2. Added bookings for multiple rooms and discount codes 
3. Designed and shipped Essential badges for loyal users 
4. Built a partnership with RadiantsDAO 
5. Crossed 17K registered users   

1.2 What's next: 
1. Platform: completed badges for multiple bookings, improve admin panel with more data, discounts, an aggregator v.2 
2. Mobile: Launch v. 10 with community verification, new badges and AI integration 
3. Nomadz 100: Cohort 2 launch campaign with 16 new creators, 

2. Highlights  
2.1 Product - Platform 
1. Improved search speed - faster results across stays, events, and nearby discovery. 
2. Added new upcoming events for Q2 2026 and continued expanding the Earth interface. 
3. Continued iterating on the referral dashboard based on user feedback.  

2.2 Product - Mobile 
1. Shipped release #10 to the Solana Mobile dApp Store: AI agent integration, new quests and badges. 
2. Release #11 in progress: Communities verification + new badges  

2.3 Growth
1. Recorded a podcast with @mango_ 
2. Were added as a travel partner to the Accelerate Miami website by @SolanaEvents
3. Sent the second email campaign: 15,000 emails delivered. 
4. Crossed 17,000+ registered users and 19,800+ followers on X. 
5. 4 weeks in a row updates provided to @DeltaV_xyz 
6. Collaborated with @sns and @SuperteamMY on the Solana Network State at @ns

 3. Lowlights  
1. We were not able to run the community campaign due to negotiations with a partner. Will launch it this week. 
2. We had a few users struggling to verify using Matrica Connect. We added a button to register.  

4. Core Metrics  
1. Total Users: 17,072 (↑ from 16,806 last week) 
2. Active Users (week): 834 (↑ from 977 last week) 
3. New Users (week):  266 (↑ from 250 last week) 
4. DAU: ~120 on average (↑ from 139 last week) 
5. Transactions on Solana (week): ~3425 (↑ from 905 last week, ~488/day) 
6. X Followers (@NOMADZxyz): 19,835+ (↑ from 19,764)  

5. This Week's Focus  
→ Launch aggregator update for platform and app on Solana dApp Store 
→ Polish badges, discount codes, events calendar with side events 
→ Scale email campaign to full 17K registered users 
→ Launch Nomadz 100 Cohort 2 and have the second community call 
→ New partnerships and launch campaigns with @cherrydotfun and @RadiantsDAO

 6. Asks  
1. Looking for content creators who are actively traveling to join Cohort 2 of Nomadz 100 - apply or refer someone  

7. Shoutouts 
Big thanks to the @solanamobile, @solana and @SolanaFndn teams for trust and support with growth. And to everyone who engaged with our content and used Nomadz.

data.translation:
most common question that comes in: "if i install Meridian and just run it, instant profit?"

short answer: no.

i can build the framework. but the edge from Meridian isn't from the framework. the edge is from you yourself.

the goal of this agent is to transfer your brain and flow in DLMM into the agent itself. strategy, preferences, way of picking tokens, way of determining range, way you exit. all that is what makes your agent become you

pastel made $102 in a day. prune $153 with 76% win rate. they're not newbies who just installed and then profited.

they're already pros at DLMM on Meteora, and what they do is transfer their knowledge into this agent. from there the agent runs on its own with the same mindset.

i asked people who get $50 and up per day from this agent. on average they play with 2 SOL capital per position, max 3 positions running at the same time.

if you don't understand DLMM at all yet, learn it first before trying Meridian. not because the agent's hard, but because you can't transfer knowledge you don't have yet.

tweets.full_text:
trashcan-sol, twice. $145 in 1 minute 8 seconds. $83 in 2 minutes 12 seconds. not my usual play but this volume was too good to ignore.

the setup: single side sol, 1 bin only. that's it.

but before anything else you need the right pool. this only works on 100/5 or 125/10 pools. anything with a 2% base fee won't give you enough to make this worthwhile.

the mechanic is simple. when your 1 bin fills and then fills back out, you're capturing roughly 2x the base fee in that single movement. on a 5% base fee pool that's around 10%, on a 10% pool you're looking at 20%+ in a single fill cycle. that's what the cards show.

volume needs to be in the 100k-200k per 5 minutes range for the bin to cycle fast enough. below that and you're just sitting there waiting.

the catch: you need to be completely locked in. eyes on the chart the whole time. when the bin fills you need to know immediately, and when it fills back out you're done, close and move on. this is not a set-and-forget play. one distraction and you miss the exit.

also prepare your mental before trying this. watching a single bin move in real time while $100+ is sitting in it is a different experience from holding a normal range position.

$228 in under 4 minutes total. gut strat for the right market conditions.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

tweets.full_text:
anime-sol, 43.78%. this is the tight bid-ask flip wide strat and it's one of my favorite plays on a strong runner.

the setup has two phases.

phase 1: tight bid-ask. you need a strong runner with huge volume and solid narrative that's already dumped around 40% from ath. 

pool link : https://t.co/Yf52Rgoclv

that's your entry window. deploy bid-ask with a tight range, -7% to -15% depending on how confident you are in the token. the goal here is simple — let your SOL convert to tokens as price sweeps through your bins on the way down.

once your SOL is fully converted to tokens, you close the position.

phase 2: flip to token side wide. open a new position using token side only with a 100-300% range to the upside. this wide range lets you DCA in and out as the token recovers. 

you're now sitting with tokens, collecting fees as the price moves back up through your range.

why not just use a normal wide range from the start? because on a strong runner with good volume the TVL gets absolutely crowded. 

tight range on the way down gets you in efficiently before the pool thickens. flipping to wide on token side gives you the recovery play without competing with everyone else for the same bins.

the whole thing only works if the narrative is genuinely strong. if the token is just a one-day wonder this falls apart at phase 2. pick the right token and the recovery almost always comes.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy
